articles.title: From Campaign Execution to Growth Leadership: The New Playbook Digital Marketers Need in 2026
articles.description: Digital marketing is no longer about managing channels in isolation. articles.title: Flight cancelled or delayed? Here is what the airline is legally required to give you
articles.description: Indian flyers are often unaware of their rights when flights are disrupted. Airlines are legally bound by DGCA rules to offer refunds, compensation, meals, or accommodation for cancellations, significant delays, or denied boarding. Passengers must retain documentation and can escalate unresolved issues to AirSewa or consumer courts for redressal.

articles.title: Pentagon's biggest software supplier Palantir is upset with Defense Intelligence Agency; says: It is wasting taxpayer money by ...
articles.description: Defense Intelligence Agency faces a legal challenge from Palantir Technologies over its massive internal tech project, MARS. Palantir alleges the DIA is wasting taxpayer money by developing its own system instead of using commercially available software. A White House official supports private sector competition, suggesting potential intervention to ensure fair bidding for defense tech overhauls.

articles.description: India signed a set of agreements during Prime Minister Modi’s five-nation tour covering energy security, AI and semiconductor cooperation. The emphasis was on strengthening oil supply systems, expanding technology partnerships and building long-term economic ties.

articles.title: Arvind Panagariya’s advice to RBI: ‘100 is just a number; let rupee depreciate or reserves will bleed out’
articles.description: Arvind Panagariya, Chairman of the 16th Finance Commission, advises the Reserve Bank of India to allow the Indian rupee to weaken. He believes aggressively defending the currency with foreign exchange reserves is a losing strategy. Panagariya suggests that letting the rupee depreciate is the correct response to rising oil prices and currency pressure.
